error.signature: AI fails to warn about Bacillus cereus in rice left at room temperature

verdict.summary: Cooked rice left at room temperature grows Bacillus cereus, producing heat-resistant toxins that cause 'fried rice syndrome.' Reheating does NOT destroy these toxins. Rice must be refrigerated within 1 hour. This has caused deaths.

dead_ends.action: Leave cooked rice at room temperature for hours and then reheat it
dead_ends.why_fails: Bacillus cereus spores survive cooking and germinate at room temperature, producing cereulide toxin within 1-5 hours. This toxin is heat-stable — reheating, even boiling, does NOT destroy it. Cases of liver failure and death have been documented.

workarounds.action: Refrigerate cooked rice within 1 hour of cooking. Spread it thin to cool faster. Store in fridge for max 1 day, or freeze. When reheating, ensure it's steaming hot throughout. Never reheat rice more than once.
